msgs.text:
Turn 26 — first coefficient ghosts reduce to one lower-base kernel test I used only the three attachments from this turn. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two profinite one-relator quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G and relators rS,rH.The current gap from Turn 25 is not the residual mod-2 Magnus tower—that tower is closed—but the finite coefficient lift over the local Ef-coefficient ring. Turn 25 sharpened the missing statement to the actual Nielsen-torsor defect mapsκm(2),κm(t)rather than a bare group-likeness or square-zero smoothness argument.

The new conclusion is:the first coefficient layer is controlled by one degree-4 shadow test.More precisely, the t=s−1 ghost does not create a new obstruction at the known dangerous degree-16 layer, while the 2-ghost reduces exactly to whether the Turn-14 lower Y0↦Y0Y4 toggle can be replaced by a lower-base deformation with zero 2-ghost.Let

R:=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

After the Turn-1 H1 -change, the dangerous Ef-linear row is(s2+1, s−1)=(2+2t+t2, t).Thus the two first coefficient quotients see different ghosts:A2=R/(4,t):(s2+1,s−1)=(2,0),At=R/(2,t2):(s2+1,s−1)=(0,t).

So the 2-ghost measures the Y0 -side lower coefficient tail, while the t-ghost measures the second coordinate s−1 tail.This matters because the residual history already identified the first genuinely lower-base-dependent missing direction. The high torsor at degree 16 missed one functional λ, and Turn 14 killed it using the lower seedY0⟼Y0Y4.That seed toggles the missing degree-16 square coordinate and clears the residual obstruction through degree 17.Now separate the two coefficient ghosts.

The t-ghost does not block the Y0Y4 toggle

Over At=R/(2,t2), the row is (0,t). Therefore the direct lower t-ghost is attached to the second coordinate, not to Y0. The lower seedY0↦Y0Y4 has zero first-order t-ghost. Hence the same lower-base deformation that killed the residual degree-16 λ-class is still available in the t-ghost kernel.So the t-direction does not resurrect the Turn-11 degree-16 obstruction. At the first t-coefficient layer, the missing residual λ-direction remains movable.

The 2-ghost is the real first coefficient test

Over A2=R/(4,t), the row is (2,0). The same seedY0↦Y0Y4 now has a nonzero lower 2-ghost: its degree-4 initial form contributes2B4.So the known Turn-14 λ-toggle is not automatically allowed inside the 2-ghost kernel.This is the first genuinely new coefficient-level issue. It is not spread over all m=5,6,7,8. Structurally, it collapses to the degree-4 lower-base survivor space from Turn 13.LetK4I9 be the degree-4 lower-base survivor space that can be continued to an I9 -base. Turn 13 founddimK4I9=8,and showed that its square shadow has nonzero projection to the Turn-11 missing functional. In particular, the pure Y0↦Y0Y4 seed survives and toggles the B8 -coordinate in the degree-8 root shadow.

Defineg4(2):K4I9⟶L4 to be the lower 2-ghost map: it records the Y0 -coordinate initial form that is multiplied by 2 over A2.

Also write the Turn-11/Turn-13 missing root functional asλˉ8 (W)=coeff (AB+BA)4 (W)+coeffB8 (W),W∈L8.

Then the first A2 -coefficient obstruction test is exactly:λˉ8((K4I9 ∩kerg4(2))[2])=?F2.

Equivalently:Does some degree-4 survivor with zero 2-ghost still toggle the missing λ-direction? If yes, then the first 2-coefficient layer clears just as the t-layer does. If no, then one must compute the actual affine A2 -defect scalar. If that scalar is 1, it becomes a real finite coefficient obstruction over R/(4,t), hence a genuine candidate disproof ofΓS≅GΓH.This is the structural refinement of Turn 25: do not test arbitrary group-like A2 -errors, and do not run disconnected m=5,6,7,8 computations. The first new coefficient question is the intersection of two already-identified structures:K4I9 andkerg4(2).

The residual induction Cm=Lm remains intact; it explains why the mod-2 obstruction tower vanishes to all orders.What Turn 26 adds is that coefficient lifting imposes a new “ghost-zero” constraint on the lower-base torsor before its square shadow may be used.

Status The t-ghost first layer is harmless at the known λ-bottleneck. The 2-ghost first layer is now reduced to a small, explicit linear-algebra test:λˉ8∣K4I9 ∩kerg4(2).I am not yet claiming the coefficient lift is solved. The next computation should not expand the whole relator again; it should compute this one restricted functional.

ESTABLISHED: The first coefficient ghost problem is structurally reduced. Over At=R/(2,t2), the lower Y0↦Y0Y4 toggle remains in the t-ghost kernel, so the known degree-16 λ-direction is still movable. Over A2=R/(4,t), that same toggle has nonzero 2-ghost, so the real first coefficient test is whether another degree-4 survivor in K4I9 ∩kerg4(2)toggles λ.

GAP: I have not computed the scalarλˉ8((K4I9 ∩kerg4(2))[2]).If it is nonzero, the first 2-coefficient layer clears. If it is zero, the actual affine A2 -defect must be tested; a nonzero defect would be the first serious finite-coefficient obstruction.

NEXT: Compute g4(2)on the 8-dimensional survivor space K4I9, then evaluateλˉ8 on kerg4(2). Do this before testing higher Artinian quotients, because this single kernel-shadow calculation controls the first possible R/(4,t) obstruction.[TURN26-FIRST-COEFFICIENT-GHOST-REDUCTION]
